MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Nov-19-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Kevin Reese, Esq. of Hanson Bridgett, et al, 415-995-5174 for defendant and moving party Steele International, Inc.
Mark Epstein, Esq., 415-863-5718 for plaintiff and opposing party Julius Steward

RULING:
ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING
DEFENDANT STEELE INTERNATIONAL, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND IS AS FOLLOWS:
Motion to vacate the default and the related discovery orders and to quash the writ of execution is granted on the conditions stated based on CCP 437(b). The conduct of counsel is responsible for the default. The motion is granted on the condition that the moving party respond to all outstanding discovery within thirty days of the date this order is signed and if plaintiff is required to file a motion to compel any further response to the discovery and the motion is granted in any material respect, this order to vacate shall be vacated upon the request of plaintiff. In addition, plaintiff shall pay attorney's fees and costs of $14,847.84. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR #8600. (302/PJM/AY)

Off Calendar Feb-08-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiff Julius Steward's motion for terminating sanctions IS Granted. No opposition has been filed. Off Calendar Oct-30-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Peter Catalonotti for plaintiff and moving party: Julius Steward

NO other APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Plaintiffs Julius Steward and Funpere Yeri-Obidake's motion to compel responses to request for production of documents is granted. No opposition has been filed. Defendant is ordered to serve complete verified responses without objections and produce documents within 15 days of notice of this order. Failure to respond was a misuse of the discovery process. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $235 are to be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. ORDER SIGNED. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
